justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified explicitly as integration risk and must-have #1; (2) API call duration gap identified in observability review and must-have #4; (3) Metric label gap identified — notes validation_requests_total has no outcome label, recommends {result="valid|invalid|error|timeout"}; (4) Latency histogram absence noted as metric gap and must-have #4; (5) PII consideration flagged appropriately — item #12 says 'mind PII — may need redaction' and places it in nice-to-have rather than blindly recommending it; (6) Verdict is NOT READY; (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable with concrete metric names, label values, and rationale.

observations.text: Request ID gap identified clearly as both an integration risk and blocking must-have with specific guidance to generate one if caller doesn't provide it
API call duration gap identified in both logging gaps and metrics, with specific recommendation for validation_duration_seconds histogram
Metric label gap identified with specific label values recommended: result="valid|invalid|error|timeout" — goes beyond the rubric's valid/invalid to include error and timeout
Latency histogram absence explicitly called out as a metric gap and must-have item
PII consideration handled correctly — input address logging placed in nice-to-have with explicit 'mind PII — may need redaction' caveat, not blindly recommended
Verdict is clearly NOT READY with well-structured blocking items
Recommendations are highly specific: named metrics (validation_duration_seconds), exact label values, concrete actions (split health check into liveness/readiness)
Excellent 3am scenario narrative that makes observability gaps tangible and convincing
Identifies error misclassification risk (SmartyStreets errors returned as 'invalid') — a subtle correctness issue beyond the rubric requirements
Appropriate escalation routing to Bird for domain correctness questions
